text.title: Swap & Memory Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Swap & Memory Management methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Swap size = min(2 × RAM, 16GB). Always have SOME swap — even 2GB beats none on a 4GB box.

rationale.source: kernel.org swap recommendations + Red Hat guidance

rule.rationale: No swap = OOM-killer triggers at the worst possible time; >16GB swap is wasted disk + thrashing risk.

rule.statement: vm.swappiness=10 on SSD-backed hosts (default 60 is for spinning disk). vm.vfs_cache_pressure=50.

rationale.source: kernel.org sysctl docs + Postgres tuning guides

rule.rationale: Higher swappiness on SSD wastes IO; 10 keeps swap for true memory pressure, not page-cache trimming.

rule.statement: Every memory-hungry systemd unit declares MemoryHigh (soft cap) AND MemoryMax (hard cap) in a drop-in. Default + 'just one service' patterns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without cgroup limits, one runaway process OOM-kills the host. MemoryHigh throttles; MemoryMax stops.

rule.statement: MemoryPlan names a single owner. 'team' / 'we' / '' are forbidden.
Alert on PSI (Pressure Stall Information) `memory.full > 10s/min` for ≥3 consecutive minutes — NOT just on OOM-kill.
rule.rationale: PSI catches degraded service before OOM-killer fires; OOM-only alerts are post-incident.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-swap-memory-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
